changelog:
Post domain-taxonomy migration cleanup: purge stale _*.md doc-naming fossils. CLAUDE.md no longer claims docs use an underscore prefix and routes planning docs to their domain folders (was the dead docs/_project-*.md path); skill-authoring describes the Doc-producer archetype with real asset to domain mappings; docs/README.md points at the domain folders + docs/.output/handoffs/; and doc-drift.js report text + comments now match the shipped domain-taxonomy behavior (the report string had it backwards, calling _project-* the canonical naming when it is the superseded legacy form).

--- v4.90 ---
Task working files now live in docs/.output/work/{date}/{task}/ (no more work/scratch/ relocation); work/ holds only the durable plan. New core tools: branch-audit (npm run branch:audit — safe branch/worktree prune audit), adr-lint (npm run adr:lint — ADR consistency over docs/architecture/decisions/), memory-backup (npm run memory:backup — out-of-repo memory-store backup).

--- v4.89 ---
docs/ DOMAIN-TAXONOMY MIGRATION (BREAKING for adopters' docs/ layout) + memory single-sourcing + /retro promotion.

HEADLINE — BREAKING for adopters: docs/ is re-homed from flat _project-*.md files + scattered folders into seven concern-domains (product/ architecture/ design/ engineering/ operations/ work/ reference/), per ADR architecture/decisions/0005-docs-domain-taxonomy. The shipped .claude/ template now reads/writes the new layout everywhere — scaffold.js, every /create:* command, all agents, doc-drift.js, and the navigation contract docs/CLAUDE.md. ADOPTER ACTION REQUIRED after this sync: re-home your own docs/ by running, from your repo root, `node .claude/core/migrate-docs-domains.js --move docs/ --apply` then `node .claude/core/migrate-docs-domains.js --refs docs/ --apply`. It is one tested, idempotent, self-verifying codemod (history-preserving git mv; --verify exits non-zero if any legacy token survives) — dry-run first by omitting --apply. If you customized your docs/ paths, review the --dry-run diff before applying.

Also in this release:
- Memory single-sourcing: per-category cap default raised 50 to 100; the 0.8 near-limit threshold and ALL relevance-scoring weights (MEMORY_SCORING: FTS + JSON-fallback paths) collapsed onto constants.js as the single source — eliminating drifting duplicate magic numbers across memory-manager, memory-guard, memory-lint, and session-start-prime.
- /review:retro promoted to a root command /retro (the old namespaced name remains only in as-of-date historical records).
- pull-request.js: spawn az/gh safely on Windows and stop masking spawn failures.
